prompt:
Storage automation ticket, Go. We group our app volumes into ONTAP consistency groups so snapshots are crash-consistent across the set, and right now that's managed by hand: someone creates the group in System Manager, someone else adds the new log volume three weeks later, and the QoS policy is whatever it happened to be that day. I want a reconciler package (module go-ontap-consistency-group, package cgrecon, stdlib only) that takes a desired spec — SVM, group name, member volumes, QoS policy — and converges the cluster to it through the documented REST surface: create the group with the member volumes when it's missing, add missing volumes / fix the QoS policy when it drifts, do nothing when it already matches, and follow the asynchronous jobs those writes return. Two hard rules from the incident review: the reconciler must never clobber fields it doesn't own (the instance document carries space stats, tags, replication info — read them, keep them in the report, never echo them into a write), and concurrent writers must be handled through the API's documented conflict signals. cg_test.go is the acceptance contract — `go test -race -timeout 30s ./...` against a loopback mock cluster; no real filer, dummy creds, no time.Sleep in the package (pacing injected).

Research first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official NetApp ONTAP REST API documentation — at minimum the application consistency-groups endpoint overview, the POST and PATCH operation references for /api/application/consistency-groups (body shapes, provisioning_options for existing volumes, qos.policy, the 202-plus-job responses, and the documented error codes for identifier conflicts and PATCH limitations), and the getting-started page plus the /api/cluster/jobs reference for the async job pattern. Only first-party NetApp documentation (docs.netapp.com, NetApp's official NetAppDocs docs source on GitHub, or library.netapp.com) counts as a source. Heads-up from my own reading: the ticket template said 'handle ETag conflicts' but ONTAP's consistency-group API doesn't document ETag/If-Match at all — confirm that against current docs, note it in your summary, and use the documented conflict error codes instead. The pinned wire contract is docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json — protected like the test file; if current docs contradict them, report the conflict rather than editing protected files. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final response.

Surface the tests compile against (split across sensible files — transport, job polling, diff, reconcile, types — your call, 3+ files):
- NewClient(baseURL, username, password string, hc *http.Client, sleep func(time.Duration)) *Client — basic auth every request; non-2xx decodes the documented error body into *APIError{Status, Code, Message, Target} whose Error() names code and message and never the password.
- Spec{SVM, Name string, Volumes []VolumeSpec{Name string}, QoSPolicy string} and Reconciler{Client *Client, MaxPolls int} with Reconcile(ctx, spec) (*Report, error); Report{UUID string, Created, Updated, NoChange bool, Restarts, JobPolls int, Final map[string]any}.
- Flow: list by name+svm.name with fields=uuid; missing -> POST with return_timeout=0 (volumes attached via provisioning_options action add, qos omitted entirely when the spec has none), poll the job on exactly its _links.self.href with sleep(1s) between polls and MaxPolls as the per-job bound ('poll budget' error naming the bound when exhausted), then re-list and read the instance with fields=** as Final. Present -> read the instance with fields=**, diff only owned fields (volumes missing from the group, QoS policy name), PATCH only the drifted keys with return_timeout=0 and follow its job, re-read as Final; nothing drifted -> NoChange with zero writes. Volumes on the group but not in the spec are left alone (the API can't remove via PATCH anyway).
- Conflicts, per the documented codes instead of ETags: a create job failing with 53411860 or an instance GET 404 with 53411842 restarts the reconcile exactly once (counted in Restarts; a second restartable condition errors with 'restart budget'); a failed PATCH job surfaces as *JobError{UUID, Code, Message}.

cg_test.go, go.mod and docs/ are protected — hands off. Finish by running the acceptance command and citing the official URLs you used.

research.product: NetApp ONTAP REST API (ONTAP 9.18.1 reference; consistency groups GA since 9.10.1)
research.interface: ONTAP REST API /api/application/consistency-groups with /api/cluster/jobs asynchronous job pattern

official_sources.title: Application consistency-groups endpoint overview (NetApp official docs source for docs.netapp.com/us-en/ontap-restapi/ontap/application_consistency-groups_endpoint_overview.html)
official_sources.used_for: collection GET with svm.name filtering and fields=*/**, instance GET /api/application/consistency-groups/{uuid}, POST/PATCH being asynchronous job operations, volumes with provisioning_options for adding existing volumes, and DELETE removing only the group while storage persists

official_sources.title: Create a consistency group (POST /api/application/consistency-groups)
official_sources.used_for: required svm.uuid|svm.name plus volumes, optional name/qos.policy/snapshot_policy, return_timeout default 0 giving 202 with a job object, and error 53411860 'An object with the same identifier in the same scope exists'

official_sources.title: Update a consistency group (PATCH /api/application/consistency-groups/{uuid})
official_sources.used_for: PATCH by uuid supporting adding volumes via provisioning_options and qos.policy.name changes, 202-plus-job responses, and the documented error codes 262285 (no element removal via PATCH), 53411842 ('Consistency group does not exist.'), 53411852/53411853 (identifier and field conflicts)

official_sources.title: Getting started with the ONTAP REST API (NetApp official docs source)
official_sources.used_for: base path /api, HTTP basic authentication, error object {error:{code,message,target}}, collection shape {records,num_records}, and the asynchronous pattern: 202 Accepted with job {uuid,_links.self.href} polled at /api/cluster/jobs/{uuid} until success or failure

official_sources.title: Retrieve details of an asynchronous job (GET /api/cluster/jobs/{uuid})
official_sources.used_for: job record fields uuid/state/message plus error {code,message} populated only on failure; success and failure are terminal

research.language_rationale: Our storage controllers are reconciled from Go operators, and ONTAP's documented Go surface is the REST API itself (NetApp maintains trident and devops-toolkit integrations in Go against this same contract). net/http with httptest exercises the documented surface hermetically with zero dependencies.
